executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Two loops that claimed full verification were relying on local self-certification instead of an enforced independent-artifact boundary. This run converted both verifier contracts to fail closed, which immediately exposed that the marketing loop still has no live independent verification artifact.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Finish the spawned independent verifier, keep the marketing loop red until it writes a fresh pass/fail artifact, then either collapse or truly split blocked-channel recovery so its two schedules stop sharing one undifferentiated owner path.

findings.title: Marketing loop now correctly fails closed because its independent-verification artifact does not exist
findings.mechanism: The marketing loop was registered as a full contract with an independent verifier, but the live verifier only became real in this run. As soon as the verifier was converted to require an independent artifact, it turned red because no such artifact was present.
findings.root_cause: The loop definition required independent verification in prose/registry, but runtime enforcement and artifact production were never wired together.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep the loop fail-closed, let the spawned independent verifier write /agents/marketing/logs/marketing_loop_independent_verification.json, and make the marketing owner path refresh that artifact continuously instead of treating verifier prose as enough.

findings.title: Architecture watchdog verifier had the same self-certification defect and now needs a fresh post-repair recheck
findings.mechanism: The architecture verifier was also just rerunning a local checker. This run converted it to an independent-artifact gate, but the currently referenced independent artifact was last checked at 2026-05-21T10:57:42Z, before the verifier-contract repair landed.
findings.root_cause: The watchdog required independent signoff conceptually, but its verifier implementation had drifted into checker replay rather than contract enforcement.
findings.recommended_fix: Refresh the independent architecture artifact so it explicitly covers the repaired verifier contract, then keep the verifier fail-closed going forward.

findings.title: Blocked-channel deep review and follow-up still share one code path and one result artifact
findings.mechanism: Two live cron schedules still invoke the same unittest + run.py path, and run.py writes a single unblock_results.json output with no mode split or separate verifier boundary.
findings.root_cause: Ownership cleanup renamed the jobs, but runtime separation never advanced beyond prompt wording.
findings.recommended_fix: Either collapse the unblocker to one owner schedule or split deep-review and follow-up into distinct entrypoints/artifacts so overlap becomes explicit and auditable.

findings.title: Disabled docs self-heal residue still leaves a stale unhealthy status artifact in the active workspace
findings.mechanism: The disabled temporary docs watchdog still leaves a visible unhealthy status file showing a fingerprint mismatch, even though the independent deactivation signoff artifact says deactivation was approved for the later clean fingerprint.
findings.root_cause: Legacy watchdog artifacts were left live in-place after authority moved to the Gateway verifier-supervisor path.
findings.recommended_fix: Archive or explicitly relabel the disabled watchdog status artifacts so future audits do not mistake them for the live docs authority path.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Complete fresh independent verification for the repaired marketing and architecture verifier contracts
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This run intentionally removed fake green behavior; the marketing loop is now honestly red until a real independent artifact exists.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The two full-contract loops will stop self-certifying and will only go green with separate evidence.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Normalize blocked-channel recovery into one explicit topology or two truly distinct ones
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: It remains the clearest live overlap/ownership ambiguity after the verifier repair.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Less duplicate churn, clearer recovery evidence, and cleaner ownership boundaries.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Archive or relabel disabled docs watchdog residue
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The stale unhealthy artifact still contaminates architectural reads even though the active authority moved elsewhere.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Lower stale-artifact confusion during future audits.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Replaced checker-rerun self-certification with a fail-closed gate that requires a fresh independent verification artifact.
Replaced checker-rerun self-certification with the same fail-closed independent-artifact gate.
Added explicit independentVerificationArtifact fields for the marketing and architecture loops so the contract is visible in the registry instead of living only in prompt prose.

Re-ran both verifier scripts after patching. Marketing now fails with a concrete missing-artifact error instead of self-certifying; architecture verifier passes only because an external artifact exists and is still within freshness.
Spawned an isolated independent verifier run to refresh the repaired verifier contracts and write/update the marketing and architecture independent verification artifacts.

independent_verification.summary: A fresh isolated verifier was spawned in this run because both verifier contracts were materially repaired. At artifact-write time, the marketing independent artifact still did not exist and the architecture independent artifact predated the verifier-contract patch.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing full-contract loop is now honestly red because no independent verification artifact exists yet
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: This is the strongest remaining proof that the old verifier contract was fake: once fail-closed enforcement was added, the loop could no longer certify itself.
